summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a radical?

A radical is a mathematical expression that uses a root symbol (√) to indicate a root of a number. The most common radical is the square root, but cube roots and higher roots are also radicals.

What is a radicand?

The radicand is the number or expression inside the radical symbol. For example, in √50, the radicand is 50. Simplifying means extracting perfect square factors from the radicand.

What is a perfect square?

A perfect square is a number that is the square of a whole number, such as 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100. Its square root is an integer.

What is the product property of radicals?

The product property of radicals states that √(a · b) = √a · √b when a and b are non-negative. This property is fundamental for simplifying radicals by breaking the radicand into factors.

What is the quotient property of radicals?

The quotient property states that √(a/b) = √a / √b when a is non-negative and b is positive. This allows you to simplify the square root of a fraction by taking the root of the numerator and denominator separately.

What is a square root?

The square root of a number n is a value that, when multiplied by itself, equals n. For example, √25 = 5 because 5 × 5 = 25. The principal square root is always non-negative.

What is the index of a radical?

The index of a radical is the small number written at the upper left of the radical symbol indicating which root to take. For square roots the index is 2 (usually omitted), for cube roots it is 3, and so on.

What is a coefficient of a radical expression?

The coefficient of a radical expression is the number multiplied in front of the radical. In 5√3, the coefficient is 5 and the radicand is 3.

A square garden has an area of 98 square feet. What is the side length in simplest radical form?

The side length is √98. Factor: 98 = 49 × 2, so √98 = √49 · √2 = 7√2 feet.

A right triangle has legs of 5 cm and 7 cm. Using the Pythagorean theorem, what is the hypotenuse in simplest radical form?

Using c² = a² + b²: c² = 25 + 49 = 74. So c = √74 cm. Since 74 = 2 × 37 has no perfect square factors, √74 is already in simplest form.

A painter needs to reach a window 12 feet high. The base of the ladder is 5 feet from the wall. What is the length of the ladder?

Using the Pythagorean theorem: c² = 12² + 5² = 144 + 25 = 169. So c = √169 = 13 feet. This is a 5-12-13 right triangle.

A kite string is taut at a length represented by √(320) feet. What is this length in simplest radical form?

Factor 320 = 64 × 5. Since 64 is a perfect square: √320 = √64 · √5 = 8√5 feet.

An engineer calculates that a support beam needs to be √(45) meters long. What is this in simplest radical form?

Factor 45 = 9 × 5. So √45 = √9 · √5 = 3√5 meters.

A recipe calls for √(12)/√(3) cups of flour. What is this amount simplified?

Use the quotient property: √12/√3 = √(12/3) = √4 = 2. The amount is exactly 2 cups.

A screen has a width of 6 inches and a height of 8 inches. What is the diagonal length?

Using the Pythagorean theorem: d = √(6² + 8²) = √(36 + 64) = √100 = 10 inches. This is a 6-8-10 right triangle (a multiple of 3-4-5).

A farmer has a field that is 5/√2 miles wide. What is the width after rationalizing the denominator?

Multiply by √2/√2: (5 · √2)/(√2 · √2) = 5√2/2 miles. The denominator is now rational.

A drone flies at a height of √(180) meters. What is this altitude in simplest radical form?

Factor 180 = 36 × 5. So √180 = √36 · √5 = 6√5 meters. The other options still contain perfect square factors under the radical.
